F1.name: Inbound delta-velocity closure under continuous-thrust electric
F1.threshold: <= 6.42 km/s impulsive-equivalent (matrix); 24.7-40.2 km/s continuous-thrust per R_inbound_dv_continuous_thrust

F2.name: B-ring crossing survivability for chunk-bearing vehicle
F2.threshold: <= 1% per-pass extended-aperture intercept; phoebe 5-round chain confirms 0/4808 cells close

F3.name: L0-05 round-trip <= 14 yr strict (or <= 15 yr waiver)

F4.name: Saturn-side delta-velocity compatible with chunk-as-propellant-tank lever
F4.threshold: <= ~3.7 km/s Saturn-side maneuvering per pre-ram-scoop conops; +14.7 km/s residence-class kills lever

F5.name: Saturn-side process power feasible (~150 kWe for 1-yr electrolysis)

F6.name: Reactor program / specific-power availability for demonstrator window 2032-2035
F6.threshold: posterior on MWe-class fission delivery by 2035: 0.07-0.20 across 3 priors; FSP Phase 2 not awarded; 0-of-6 US base rate

F7.name: Foundational-lever consistency: cargo serves as inbound propellant tank

F8.name: Capital-class threshold (tech-demonstrator OR better; NOT venture-required)
F8.threshold: tech-demonstrator anchor per iapetus settlement; venture requires 5-fold >= 0.93 joint priors (1378x baseline lift)

candidates.name: Held chunk-rendezvous
candidates.description: Bag-rendezvous with single B-ring chunk; matrix baseline before phoebe 5-round chain.

cells.rationale: Continuous-thrust inbound 24.7-40.2 km/s exceeds matrix 6.42 baseline by 3.8-6.3x; surviving 200-t cell at 24.1% delivered fraction only.
Phoebe 5-round chain: 0 of 4,808 unique closure-checks pass extended-aperture survivability threshold at any (threshold, mesh, aperture, chunk-mass, q-exponent) combination.
14-yr ceiling achievable per R12 lunar-GA 13.91 yr at 70% delivery (different sub-architecture but same chunk capture).
Inbound chunk-rendezvous preserves chunk-as-propellant-tank lever (the foundational case).
Saturn-side process power independent of capture-architecture; surface mining vs ring rendezvous changes the calculus.
Variant-C closure requires reactor program at megawatt class; posterior 0.07-0.20; FSP Phase 2 not awarded; 0-of-6 US fission base rate.
Tech-demonstrator anchor reachable conditional on engineering; iapetus settled program-class.

candidates.name: Chunk-rendezvous + deployable drag-skirt aerocapture
candidates.description: Inflatable ballute / HIAD-class deployable drag-skirt for Earth aerocapture closure on chunk-bearing vehicle.
candidates.aggregate_rationale: Fails F1, F2, F6, F8 (physical-kill criteria)

cells.rationale: Same as A; aerocapture closure does not change continuous-thrust inbound penalty during cruise.
Same as A; drag-skirt addresses Earth aerocapture only, not B-ring rendezvous survivability.
Drag-skirt enables aerocapture saves 1.5-3.5 km/s on Earth approach; could relax round-trip but doesn't change inbound cruise.
R_deployable_drag_skirt: peak heat flux 1,431 kW/m^2 at best-case (beta=100, areal=15) is 3-4x HIAD-2 design and LOFTID demonstrated; thermal does not close.

candidates.name: Outer-ring chunk-rendezvous (A-ring or F-ring)
candidates.description: Same architecture as A but rendezvous with chunks in A-ring (tau~0.5) or F-ring (tau~0.1) rather than B-ring (tau~2.0).

cells.rationale: Same as A; inbound cruise penalty independent of ring location.
B-ring zone-averaged tau ~2.0 kills A; F-ring tau ~0.1 reduces per-pass intercept by ~20x, but F-ring chunk-population is much lower and constituent particles are micron-class.
cells.cited_source: Cassini ring optical-depth data; needs new round

cells.rationale: Outer-ring approach Δv differs marginally from B-ring; not directly tested.
Conditional on F2 closure; if F-ring chunk-population is too low, capital-class question is moot.

candidates.name: Aerocapture-conditional chunk-rendezvous (single-pass)
candidates.description: Chunk-as-heat-shield single-pass aerocapture per R_megawatt_aerocapture_engineering_closure.

cells.rationale: Aerocapture-conditional: round-trip 4.81 yr (continuous-thrust); delivered fraction 42.7% at 200-t chunk.
Same as A; aerocapture closure does not change B-ring rendezvous.
4.81 yr round-trip clears L0-05 by ~9 yr.
Required reactor still 1000 kWe (10x FSP Phase 2); posterior 0.10-0.30 by 2032-35.
Conditional on F6 closure; tech-demonstrator capital class reachable.

candidates.name: Ram-scoop residence-class (B-ring)
candidates.description: Open-bag accretion at v_rel ~10 m/s during residence in B-ring; +14.7 km/s Saturn-side circularisation Δv.

cells.rationale: Inbound continuous-thrust same penalty as A; not the binding constraint here.
Residence-class avoids the rendezvous-crossing problem (operates at orbital-matched velocity).

titan-2 Block 5/9 found composite steady-state delivered fraction 15.97% (Option-A-equivalent) at ~16 yr; Block 10 caught exit-burn-power audit showing 500-kWe nominal exit burn takes 8.92 yr (17.8x assumed 6-month dwell).
Residence-class +14.7 km/s Saturn-side Δv directly defeats chunk-as-propellant-tank lever; project-owner explicit retirement.
Bulk ring material provides electrolysis feedstock; same Saturn-side power class as chunk processing.
Residence-class exit burn requires MWe-class power per titan-2 Block 10 (8.9 MWe at Isp 7000 for 6-month exit).
Bulk ring material is not coherent chunk; cannot serve as cohesive propellant tank.
Tech-demonstrator capital-class reachable conditional on lever-reframe.

candidates.name: Outer-ring residence-class (A-ring or F-ring)
candidates.description: Ram-scoop architecture in outer ring; same Δv penalty as B; lower particle density.

cells.rationale: Same as B; residence not rendezvous.
Same as B; outer-ring fill time orders-of-magnitude longer at lower density.
Same as B; circularisation penalty unchanged at outer-ring radius.

candidates.name: Lunar-orbit catcher
candidates.description: Saturn-side chunk handoff to inbound-delivery stage; chunks intercepted at lunar orbit rather than LEO.

cells.rationale: Chunks still need to be moved from Saturn to lunar orbit; same inbound penalty as A unless capture-physics is different.
Chunk acquisition at Saturn still requires B-ring rendezvous unless paired with a non-chunk-rendezvous source.
Delivery to lunar orbit may relax Earth-arrival Δv by ~0.7-1.2 km/s but only ~5.2 pp delivered fraction per R_delivery_destination_altitude.

candidates.name: Tether-rendezvous (rotating tether passive grapple)
candidates.description: Spin-tether momentum-exchange grapple of chunk during B-ring crossing.

cells.rationale: Tether must transit B-ring twice (deploy + retract) at high relative velocity; same intercept problem.

candidates.name: Active push-the-rock (lander + boost stage on chunk)
candidates.description: Land on chunk in B-ring; mount boost stage; thrust chunk into Earth-return trajectory.

cells.rationale: Same inbound penalty applies; chunk acceleration is mass-ratio-bound by chosen propellant class.
Landing requires sustained relative-velocity matching in B-ring; transit problem unchanged.

candidates.name: Enceladus plume sampling
candidates.description: Water from south-pole geysers (~200 kg/s aggregate plume mass flow); no ring crossing required.

cells.rationale: Enceladus orbit (~4 R_S) is outside B-ring but inside E-ring; Saturn-side delta-velocity to Enceladus orbital insertion plus departure not in matrix; needs new round.
Enceladus is at 4 Saturn radii; outside dense rings entirely. E-ring is diffuse ice-grain torus generated by plumes; tau is ~10^-5.
Saturn-side dwell to collect 200-t-equivalent at ~0.1-1 kg/s effective capture from plume mass flow ~10^2-10^5 yr; needs new round.
Enceladus orbital insertion adds Δv but bulk water acquisition replaces chunk-rendezvous; lever-consistency depends on collection rate.
Saturn-side process power same class as A; not architecture-changing.
Same reactor-program constraint as A unless inbound is solved differently.
Conditional on collected mass packaging: if collected as ice slurry / aggregate, chunk-as-propellant-tank lever lost (similar to F2d/B); if as solid block, lever preserved.

candidates.name: Mimas surface ice mining
candidates.description: Land on Mimas (3.1 R_S, water-ice surface), surface-mine, ascend.

cells.rationale: Saturn-side Δv to Mimas orbit + descent/ascent (~0.4-0.8 km/s surface escape) + return; not in matrix.
Mimas is outside main ring system (just inside E-ring); ring crossing minimal.
Mining cadence at ICEBERG-scale (200-t/yr to 200-t/decade) unknown; surface mining heritage zero at this mass scale.
Surface-mined ice block could serve as chunk-as-propellant-tank if packaged cohesively; processing changes the calculus.

candidates.name: Iapetus surface ice mining
candidates.description: Land on Iapetus (59 R_S, dark/light hemisphere); surface ice abundant on light hemisphere.

cells.rationale: Iapetus orbit at 59 R_S is far outside Saturn's main system; Saturn-side cruise adds ~6 months one-way; mass-ratio cost not in matrix but adds Δv comparable to titan Block 9 finding (~10.49 yr powered cruise close-time).
Iapetus is well outside all rings.
Iapetus's 79-day orbital period + slow return synodic windows + mining cadence push round-trip to >>14 yr.

candidates.name: Hyperion surface ice mining (porous body)
candidates.description: Hyperion is a porous tumbling body at 24.6 R_S; sponge-like surface may permit easy ice extraction.

cells.rationale: Hyperion orbit at 24.6 R_S; Saturn-side cruise penalty intermediate.
Mining cadence on porous chaotically-tumbling body unprecedented.

candidates.name: Tethys surface ice mining
candidates.description: Tethys (4.9 R_S, ~99% water-ice composition); shallow gravity well.

cells.rationale: Tethys orbit close to Saturn (4.9 R_S); Saturn-side Δv modest.
Just outside E-ring; minimal ring crossing.

candidates.name: Phoebe surface ice (irregular satellite)
candidates.description: Phoebe at 215 R_S, retrograde orbit, comet-class composition (high water-ice fraction).

cells.rationale: Phoebe at 215 R_S in retrograde orbit; Saturn-side cruise and orbital insertion costs are enormous (functionally similar to S7 Trojans).
Saturn-side dwell + ~4-month one-way cruise + return synodic windows pushes round-trip > 20 yr.

candidates.name: Saturn-system Trojan / Hilda water-ice
candidates.description: Bodies at Saturn-Sun L4/L5 (or Hilda 2:3 with Jupiter); high water content but distant.

cells.rationale: Trojans at 5-9 AU; transit time + Saturn departure penalty vs current direct-to-Saturn architecture is strictly worse.
Round-trip to Saturn-Trojan and back > 24 yr per R9 analogy.

candidates.name: Very slow low-energy cruise
candidates.description: Trade transit time for delta-velocity (long Hohmann-class transfers).

cells.rationale: Slow cruise relaxes inbound Δv requirement.
B-ring rendezvous problem unchanged (slow cruise doesn't affect ring transit).
R9 slow trajectory: realistic round-trip ~24 yr; conops 13-yr triple internally inconsistent.
Lower power Kilopower-class might close if Δv is small enough; R9 shows it doesn't.

candidates.name: Chunk pre-staging at intermediate moon
candidates.description: Pre-stage chunks in Iapetus or Mimas parking orbit; phase-2 push to Earth-return.

cells.rationale: Two-stage architecture: Saturn-side stage moves chunk to parking; phase-2 stage handles inbound. Each stage has lower Δv but stages don't compose linearly.
Chunk acquisition step still requires B-ring rendezvous.
Pre-staging breaks the 14-yr cap into two-mission cycle; commit-timing problem changes (similar to R_heterogeneous_cadence's adjacent staged-commitment hypothesis).

candidates.name: Return propellant (H2/O2) not water (Architecture D)
candidates.description: Saturn-side electrolyse water into hydrogen + oxygen; return cryogenic propellant.

cells.rationale: Electrolysed propellant changes inbound mass-ratio math.
R_architecture_D_cost: requires 10 W/kg FSP Phase 1 STRETCH (not contracted 5 W/kg); 20-t reactor 200 kWe.
Same reactor-program constraint, plus needs 200-kWe Saturn-side process power class.
Returning propellant not water means cargo is not the propellant tank; lever lost.

R_architecture_D_cost: 0 of 48 cells return defined IRR. R_architecture_D_L1007_relaxation: program-NPV negative across 168 cells (D-fission $-15.6B, D-solar $-14.1B at best cell vs Variant B $+42.3B).

candidates.name: Return as bulk ring material aggregate
candidates.description: Collect bulk ring material at Saturn (ram-scoop output) and return as aggregate.

candidates.name: Many small chunks instead of one big
candidates.description: Heterogeneous cadence with chunk_1 << 200 t to lower mission-1 commit cost.
candidates.aggregate_rationale: Fails F1, F2, F3, F6, F8 (physical-kill criteria)

cells.rationale: Same as A; inbound penalty unchanged.
Same as A; rendezvous problem multiplies per chunk.
R_heterogeneous_cadence: all chunk_1 < 200 t lose $30M-$113M NPV in regime D, sacrificed $484M in regime R. R_cadence_multiship: compressed N>=3 collapses IRR to -1.45%.

candidates.name: Deliver to L4/L5/GEO/lunar-orbit instead of LEO
candidates.description: Different terminal delivery altitude.
candidates.aggregate_rationale: Fails F1, F2, F6, F8 (physical-kill criteria)

cells.rationale: R_delivery_destination_altitude: only 5.2 pp gain LEO -> GEO; not architecture-changing per IRR/NPV.

candidates.name: Precursor mission (smaller water + sell heritage)
candidates.description: Smaller target water mass; revenue model leans on heritage value to follow-on architectures.

cells.rationale: Smaller chunk relaxes mass-ratio; could close inbound.
Even small chunk requires B-ring rendezvous.
Smaller chunk could fly faster (lower mass-ratio).
Smaller chunk reduces reactor-power requirement; Kilopower-class may close.
Capital structure depends on heritage revenue model not water-revenue.

candidates.name: Shared launch / cost-split with other Saturn customers
candidates.description: Outbound launch costs split with science / NASA missions; reduces 6.9x launch-mass multiplier impact.

cells.rationale: Shared-launch could rescue per-mission economics conditional on actual co-customer existing.

candidates.name: Data-resource mode (smaller water + sensor-data primary revenue)
candidates.description: Smaller water mass; revenue from in-situ science + heritage data sales; water as proof-of-concept.

cells.rationale: Smaller water target relaxes reactor power.
Capital structure: science revenue is not subject to launch-cost displacement competition (per existing belief 'parallel science revenue stream').

candidates.name: One-way ships (no return)
candidates.description: Deliver chunk and abandon vehicle in cislunar / Earth-vicinity; no Saturn-return mission cycle.

cells.rationale: One-way Tsiolkovsky drops mass-ratio significantly; could close at lower Isp.
Round-trip not applicable; only one-way transit time.
Vehicle disposal cost vs reuse cost not yet modeled; outbound launch cost unchanged.

candidates.name: Tug-and-go fleet (one tug per chunk; tug stays at Saturn)
candidates.description: Dedicated Saturn-side tug per chunk-acquisition; tugs never return.

cells.rationale: Tug doesn't perform inbound; separate inbound stage required.
Per-mission reactor cost multiplied by tug count; cadence-economics collapse per R_cadence_multiship.
R_cadence_multiship: N=5 fleet drops IRR to -1.45%; compressed cadence is anti-optimal at N>=3.

candidates.name: Drop 14-yr round-trip cap (relax L0-05)
candidates.description: L0-05 waiver opens architectures with > 14 yr round-trip.

cells.rationale: B-ring rendezvous problem orthogonal to L0-05.
Capital-class implications of longer round-trip not in iapetus framework.

candidates.name: Drop chunk-as-propellant-tank premise (relax F7)
candidates.description: Foundational lever relaxation; enables ram-scoop B / F2d / S1-aggregate variants.

candidates.name: Drop 100% Earth-delivery target
candidates.description: Allow inbound mass-ratio to leave residual in-flight propellant; deliver less to Earth.

cells.rationale: Lower delivered mass relaxes reactor power per R_chunk_fed_chemical mapping.
Chunk-as-propellant-tank preserved (the chunk is the propellant supply).
Revenue model implications; partial-delivery economics not yet modeled.

candidates.name: Drop 'Earth orbit' delivery target (lunar / L4-L5 only)
candidates.description: Deliver to cislunar depot, not LEO; serves propellant-economy customers not water-end-users.

cells.rationale: R_delivery_destination_altitude: only 5.2 pp delta LEO->GEO; cislunar marginally better; not architecture-saving on capital-class.
